text: Managing a team effectively requires several key skills. First, clear communication is essential so everyone understands their tasks and goals. Good delegation means assigning the right work to the right people, trusting them to complete it, and avoiding micromanagement. Regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued. Motivation is also crucial; recognizing achievements and encouraging growth keeps morale high. Finally, conflict resolution must be handled calmly and fairly, addressing disagreements before they harm teamwork. By focusing on these areas, a manager can create a productive, positive environment where everyone contributes to success.
